data.title: Northrop Grumman's unit wins $475M contract modification
data.description:
• Northrop Grumman Systems, a wholly owned subsidiary of Northrop Grumman (NOC), received a contract modification worth $475.3M, increasing the total deal value to $1.31B.
  • The project focuses on developing a Glide Phase Interceptor missile system on an accelerated timeline.
  • The work is expected to be completed by June 2028.
  • The total of $174.1M in funding has been allocated at the time of the award, and the Missile Defense Agency is managing the contract.

data.title: Cytophage COO departs following restructuring
data.description:
• Cytophage Technologies (CYTO:CA) on Tuesday said its chief operating officer, Heather Medwick, left the company effective March 31 following a corporate restructuring.
  • The company said the restructuring is aimed at streamlining operations, reducing costs and preserving financial resources.
  • Cytophage added that a special committee continues to evaluate strategic alternatives, including a potential financing, with no timeline set for a decision.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Alimentation Couche-Tard prices private debt offering
data.description:
• Alimentation Couche-Tard (ATD:CA) has priced a private debt offering of Euro denominated senior unsecured notes due 2033 with an aggregate principal amount of €750M.
  • The notes will carry a coupon of 3.901% per annum and will be sold at par.
  • The offering of the notes is expected to close on or about April 21, 2026.
  • ANCTF +0.48% after hours to $56.20.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Broadcom edges up after inking three-year deal with Meta to help develop AI chips
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) has entered into a three-year partnership with Meta Platforms (META) to help the company develop its artificial intelligence chips and deploy them into data centers, the companies announced today.

Shares of Broadcom had edged up 3% during early post-market trading on Tuesday. 

Under the deal, Broadcom will deliver technology supporting Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) chips through 2029. The initial commitment is more than 1GW and is the first phase of a sustained, multi-gigawatt rollout.

"This initial MTIA deployment is just the beginning of a sustained, multi-generation roadmap to serve the trajectory of massive growth over the next few years, highlighting Broadcom's unmatched leadership in AI networking and the power of our foundational XPU custom accelerator platform," said Broadcom CEO Hock Tan.

"Meta is partnering with Broadcom across chip design, packaging, and networking to build out the massive computing foundation we need to deliver personal superintelligence to billions of people," said Meta founder and CEO Mark Zuckerberg. 

As part of the partnership agreement, Tan will transition from Meta's Board of Directors and into an advisory role for Meta. He will "provide guidance on Meta's custom silicon roadmap and help shape the future of their infrastructure investments."

Last week, Broadcom signed a long-term deal with Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) to develop its custom AI chips, known as tensor processing units. That deal runs through 2031.

data.title: Gold gains on weaker dollar, sub-$100 oil as hopes build for new U.S.-Iran talks
data.description:
Gold futures gained Tuesday after a two-day decline, as the dollar continued to drift lower and crude oil dropped below $100/bbl on hopes of further dialogue between the U.S. and Iran, which supported prices by easing inflation concerns.

President Trump said talks to ​end the Middle East war could resume in Pakistan over the next two days, ​after the collapse of weekend negotiations prompted ⁠the U.S. to impose a naval blockade on Iranian ports.

"Any signs of diplomatic progress may help ease inflation concerns and support a recovery in bullion," Exness analyst Inki Cho said in a note. "Gold's direction will remain closely tied to developments in the Middle East and their impact on energy prices and inflation expectations."

"As long as the market does not begin to seriously consider a rate hike by the U.S. Federal Reserve—there are no signs of this so far—the gold price is unlikely to fall much further," Commerzbank analyst Carsten Fritsch said in a note.

Despite a modest recovery in recent weeks, bullion has still fallen ~10% since the start of the Middle East war in late February, as a liquidity squeeze in the earlier days of fighting saw investors offloading their gold holdings to cover losses elsewhere.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery rose 1.7% to $4,825.00/oz, and front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) jumped 5.1% to $79.391/oz, the best settlement values for both precious metals since mid-March.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Citi targets 10%-11% 2026 RoTCE while keeping efficiency ratio around 60% and U.S. card NCL rate at 4%-4.5%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Citigroup (C) Q1 2026

Management view

  • Jane Fraser said Citi had “an exceptionally strong start to 2026,” reporting “net income of $5.8 billion for the first quarter with an EPS of $3.06 and an RoTCE of 13.1%,” while “overall revenues were up sharply at 14%” and the firm delivered “very healthy positive operating leverage.”

  • Fraser highlighted Services as “our crown jewel,” citing “new mandates were up 40%,” “a 17% increase in revenues,” “deposits grew by 16%,” and “assets under custody and administration were up over 20%.”

  • Fraser said Markets “crossed $7 billion in revenues for the first time in a decade,” with “Equities… up nearly 40%, surpassing the $2 billion revenue mark,” and “FICC, up 13%,” while Banking had “fees up 12% amidst a record first quarter for us in M&A” and “ECM was up over 60%.”

  • Fraser said Citi repurchased “$6.3 billion of shares,” is “close to completing our $20 billion share buyback plan,” and ended the quarter with “a CET1 ratio of 12.7%… 110 basis points above our regulatory capital requirement.”

  • Gonzalo Luchetti (Chief Financial Officer Gonzalo Luchetti) said, “This quarter, we reported net income of $5.8 billion, EPS of $3.06 and an RoTCE of 13.1%, on $24.6 billion of revenues,” and detailed “expenses of $14.3 billion” and “cost of credit was $2.8 billion.”

Outlook

  • Luchetti said Citi’s “overall expectations are unchanged,” and “subject to macro and market conditions,” the company still expects “NII ex-Markets up approximately 5% to 6%,” “an efficiency ratio of around 60%,” and a “total U.S. credit card NCL rate of between 4% and 4.5%.”

  • On capital return, Luchetti said Citi “plan[s] to provide more detail on our expectations for share repurchases going forward at our Investor Day in May,” while Fraser reiterated the firm remains “well on track to deliver the 10% to 11% RoTCE for the year.”

  • Compared with Q4 2025, management kept the same full-year framing around “deliver the 10% to 11% RoTCE” and “efficiency ratio of around 60%,” but updated credit language by specifying the all-in U.S. card NCL rate as “between 4% and 4.5%.”

Financial results

  • Luchetti reported total revenues of “$24.6 billion,” with “total revenues… up 14%,” “expenses of $14.3 billion… up 7%,” and an “efficiency ratio of 58%,” alongside “cost of credit… $2.8 billion,” including “a firm-wide net ACL build of $597 million.”

  • Luchetti said the quarter included “nearly $500 million of severance,” and added, “Excluding severance, the increase in expenses was 4%,” while the efficiency ratio improved “approximately 400 basis points” on the revenue growth.

  • On reserves, Luchetti said Citi ended the quarter with “nearly $22 billion in total reserves” and “a reserve-to-funded loans ratio of 2.6%,” with reserves incorporating “an 8-quarter weighted average unemployment rate of approximately 5.4%… [including] a downside scenario… nearly 7%.”

  • For capital and balance sheet, Luchetti reported “total assets of $2.8 trillion,” a “$1.4 trillion deposit base,” “$6.3 billion in buybacks,” and a “12.7% CET1 ratio… approximately 110 basis points above the 11.6% regulatory capital requirement.”

Q&A

  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore ISI: asked about Services growth and tokenization, including “the $4 trillion win on the BlackRock middle office” and whether tokenization is “a good thing as opposed to maybe the threat”; CEO Fraser said Services growth is driven by “deepening with existing clients, new client acquisition and new product innovations,” adding, “in digital assets, we are leading in tokenization.”

  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore ISI: asked about U.S. retail banking acquisition aspirations; CEO Fraser said, “We are only interested in and focused on organic growth, period, end of story,” and later added, “we’re not going to be distracted from it.”

  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: pressed on whether acquisitions are “1,000% off the table”; CEO Fraser responded, “we are not interested in anything other than organic growth.”

  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: asked about the “last 10%” of transformation; CEO Fraser said the remaining work is “primarily related to data used in our regulatory reporting,” and emphasized, “they control the time line” on regulatory closure.

  • John McDonald, Truist: asked about Basel/G-SIB impact; CFO Luchetti said Citi expects a “moderate net benefit,” and reiterated, “Moderate net benefit.”

  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA: questioned why Q1 RoTCE “should go down to the 10% to 11% range”; CEO Fraser said, “One good-rate first quarter does not a full year make,” citing uncertainty and continued investment.

  • James Mitchell, Seaport: asked about low Retail Bank profitability and private credit exposure; CFO Luchetti pointed to Wealth RoTCE of “10.8%” and said private credit exposure is “$22 billion… 98% investment-grade,” describing it as “not a significant exposure for us.”

  • Manan Gosalia, Morgan Stanley: asked about CTA and Banamex; CFO Luchetti said deconsolidation would bring “about an $8.5 billion or so CTA adjustment… but in aggregate, it’s capital neutral.”

  • David Chiaverini, Jefferies: asked about Banking pipeline and Banamex IPO; CFO Luchetti said the M&A pipeline “continues to be quite strong,” but warned a prolonged conflict “may start introducing some risk of deferrals”; CEO Fraser said Citi does “not anticipate any additional stake sales in 2026 ahead of deconsolidation in early 2027,” and the IPO would “most likely… happen after that when market conditions are favorable.”

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was slightly negative to pressing on M&A rumors, capital rules specificity, and sustainability of returns (e.g., Mayo’s “1,000% off the table” framing and Poonawala’s challenge on why returns would decline).

  • Management’s tone was confident in performance and strategy, and firm on constraints: Fraser repeatedly used “crystal clear” language on organic growth and emphasized that regulators “control the time line” for consent order closure.

  • Versus Q4 2025, the Q1 2026 exchange showed more explicit, repeated management pushback on acquisition speculation, while continuing a consistent message that transformation progress is substantial but final closure timing sits with regulators.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Q1 2026 introduced a CFO changeover, with Luchetti opening by thanking “Jane and Mark for their support” in a “smooth transition,” following Mark Mason’s final call in Q4 2025.

  • Citi’s messaging on transformation advanced from Q4 2025 “over 80% of our programs are now at or nearly at our target state” to Q1 2026 “90% of our programs are now at or near our target state,” while both quarters stressed that regulators determine closure timing.

  • Capital return emphasis increased sequentially in tone and specificity, with Q1 2026 highlighting “$6.3 billion” of buybacks and being “close to completing our $20 billion share buyback plan,” while Q4 2025 centered on the annual pace and the $20 billion program framework.

  • Analyst focus shifted from Q4’s heavier emphasis on transformation/efficiency mechanics and card regulation issues to Q1’s concentration on Services durability and wins, acquisition rumors, Basel/G-SIB impacts, and capital deployment around divestitures.

Risks and concerns

  • Fraser flagged macro risks tied to geopolitics and inflation: “the impact of the Middle East conflict is hitting Asia and Europe harder,” and “inflation is now a greater risk to growth and will likely cause central banks to lean towards more restrictive monetary policies.”

  • Luchetti said reserves were adjusted with “a further skew to the downside scenario,” reflecting “increased uncertainty in the macroeconomic outlook,” and added that ACL levels will remain “a function of the macroeconomic environment and business volumes.”

  • Management identified regulatory and capital-rule uncertainty as an active area, with Fraser saying the latest NPR “is an improvement… it’s not yet where it should be,” and the firm would be “active in advocating for necessary changes.”

Final takeaway

Management framed Q1 2026 as a strong start driven by double-digit revenue growth in four core businesses, with standout momentum in Services and Markets, while reiterating full-year targets of 10%-11% RoTCE, NII ex-Markets growth of about 5%-6%, an efficiency ratio around 60%, and a 4%-4.5% U.S. card net credit loss rate. Leadership repeatedly emphasized organic growth only, highlighted ongoing capital return and divestiture execution (including Banamex timing and Russia exit), and positioned transformation progress as largely complete but still subject to regulatory validation and timing.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: CEMATRIX renews share buyback program
data.description:
• CEMATRIX Corporation (TSX: CEMX) (OTCQB: CTXXF) (CEMX:CA) on Tuesday said it has received approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange to renew its normal course issuer bid.
  • The company said it may repurchase up to 13.37 million common shares, representing about 10% of its public float, starting April 17, 2026, through April 16, 2027.
  • CEMATRIX said the buyback will be funded with existing cash and is aimed at enhancing shareholder value.
  • CTXXF closed +3.56% at $0.3779.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ASML options price 6% earnings move as traders favor upside
data.description:
Options traders are positioning for a sizable move in ASML (ASML) shares following the semiconductor equipment maker’s first-quarter 2026 earnings report, scheduled for early Wednesday.

As of Tuesday's close, ASML’s (ASML) April 17 weekly options chain suggests the market is pricing in a post-earnings move of about 6.3%, translating to a market-implied trading range of roughly $1,420 to $1,610. With the stock trading around $1,518 a share, straddles at nearby strikes were valued at $95 to $97.

What stands out in the options activity is heavy trading in the belly of the chain on both sides—typically a sign of event-volatility positioning rather than simple directional bets. The $1,500 calls saw 542 contracts trade against 1,454 open interest, while 1,520 calls traded 586 contracts against just 273 open interest.

The wing activity tells an interesting story: traders appear more willing to chase upside than position for disaster.

Call volume was notably heavy at strikes well above spot, including the $1,600 and $1,700 levels. Downside protection was present but concentrated at lower strikes like $1,400 and $1,440, suggesting traders want some insurance but aren’t betting on a collapse near current levels.

data.title: NN sees Q1 beat, lifts 2026 new business outlook to $80–90M range
data.description:
• NN (NNBR) announced on Tuesday that its preliminary Q1 2026 net sales are expected to show YoY growth and come in ahead of its internal forecast.
  • The company's new business program delivered strong Q1 results and secured a ~$43M peak annual sales award, driven by electric grid and data center demand.
  • Following this, the company has raised its full-year 2026 guidance to $80M–$90M, reflecting continued strength into early Q2.
  • Moreover, more than 60 new programs were launched, creating a shippable backlog, as orders exceeded production during the quarter.
  • NN (NNBR) kept its $445M–$465M net sales guidance, now expected in the upper half, while shifting toward higher non-auto business.
  • CEO Harold Bevis said, We are currently ahead of our 2026 and long-term targets and will share more details in the Q1 2026 earnings release scheduled for May 6, 2026, as the company is still finalizing final results.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OpenAI releases new Cyber model one week after Anthropic's Mythos Preview made waves
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) revealed its GPT-5.4-Cyber today, exactly one week after Anthropic (ANTHRO) made waves with the public revelation of its Claude Mythos Preview, which also featured advanced cybersecurity capabilities.

The advent of Mythos prompted a range of cybersecurity stocks to fall. However, these were mostly static during early post-market trading on Tuesday.

Similar to Anthropic's release of Mythos, which was only shared with about 40 big tech companies, OpenAI is also being selective with who gains access to Cyber. The model will be available for some members of OpenAI's Trusted Access for Cyber program. Individual users and enterprises can apply to join the program.

"All customers approved through this process will gain access to versions of existing models with reduced friction around safeguards which might trigger on dual-use cyber activity, allowing them to continue to support security education, defensive programming, and responsible vulnerability research," OpenAI said.

While AI tools pose greater threats to software systems, they can also help identify threats and vulnerabilities. OpenAI plans to allow more users to test Cyber's capabilities in the weeks and months ahead.

"Digital infrastructure has already been vulnerable for years, before advanced AI even came along," OpenAI said. "Now, existing models can help find vulnerabilities, reason across codebases, and support meaningful parts of the cyber workflow, and threat actors are experimenting with novel AI-driven approaches. We've seen sophisticated harnesses elicit stronger and stronger capabilities by using more test-time compute with existing models. That means safeguards cannot wait for a single future threshold."
